case_title: Escalating administrative delay with partial institutional response
decision_trigger: A local authority provided a partial procedural response after repeated delay but did not resolve the substantive issue.
recall_question: What evidence supports the pattern of delay, partial response, and escalation readiness?
user_priority: timeline clarity, evidence linkage, administrative readiness
desired_outcome: A complete substantive response, clear ownership of the issue, and an auditable evidence record for escalation.
rules_or_procedures: External ombudsman referral after internal delay or incomplete response
constraints: Partial response did not address the substantive issue
Responsibility was redirected between service and complaints teams
Escalation requires a clear chronology and supporting evidence
The complainant must avoid creating duplicate complaint threads

actors.name: Citizen complainant
actors.role_in_case: documenting delay, requesting a substantive response, and preparing escalation
actors.incentive_or_pressure: needs resolution without losing procedural footing
actors.likely_behaviour: maintain chronology, preserve correspondence, and link evidence to each unresolved target

actors.name: Housing case officer
actors.role_in_case: initial service owner for the substantive issue
actors.incentive_or_pressure: manage operational workload and avoid accepting unresolved responsibility
actors.likely_behaviour: provide limited updates and redirect formal complaint handling

actors.name: Complaints team
actors.role_in_case: issuing partial procedural response and managing escalation route
actors.incentive_or_pressure: close procedural steps while limiting scope of admission
actors.likely_behaviour: acknowledge delay, describe process, but leave substantive remedy unresolved

actors.name: External Local Government Ombudsman
actors.role_in_case: potential external review route if internal response remains incomplete
actors.incentive_or_pressure: require evidence of completed or stalled internal process
actors.likely_behaviour: request chronology, correspondence, decision records, and proof of unresolved complaint outcome

evidence_bundle.label: Initial service request and complaint
evidence_bundle.relevance: Establishes the starting point for the administrative delay and the substantive issue raised

evidence_bundle.label: Acknowledgement without substantive answer
evidence_bundle.relevance: Shows partial institutional response without resolving the underlying issue

evidence_bundle.label: Follow-up chronology
evidence_bundle.relevance: Documents repeated contact without resolution and supports evidence coverage review

evidence_bundle.label: Responsibility redirection email
evidence_bundle.relevance: Shows the issue was redirected between service and complaints functions

evidence_bundle.label: Stage 2 escalation draft
evidence_bundle.relevance: Frames the unresolved targets for administrative evidence review through outcome, resolution, closure, and archive stages

deadlines.label: Stage 1 substantive response deadline
deadlines.consequence: Missed deadline supports institutional delay and escalation readiness

deadlines.label: Stage 2 escalation preparation date
deadlines.consequence: Escalation package should be evidence-linked before external review

timeline.label: Initial complaint submitted
timeline.description: The complainant submitted a formal complaint to the local authority housing service after prolonged inaction on a service issue.

timeline.label: Partial acknowledgement received
timeline.description: The authority acknowledged receipt and referenced the complaints process but did not answer the substantive issue.

timeline.label: Repeated follow-up contacts recorded
timeline.description: Multiple follow-up contacts were made after no substantive decision or remedy was provided.

timeline.label: Responsibility redirected
timeline.description: The service team redirected responsibility to the complaints team while the complaints team referred back to service ownership.

timeline.label: Escalation prepared after partial response
timeline.description: The complainant prepared a Stage 2 escalation after the authority issued only a partial procedural response and no substantive remedy.

escalation_paths.name: Stage 2 internal complaint escalation
escalation_paths.trigger: Partial procedural response did not resolve the substantive issue
escalation_paths.likely_response: A further procedural review or request for additional chronology
escalation_paths.outcome_note: Acknowledgement and process explanation received, but substantive resolution remains outstanding

escalation_paths.name: External Local Government Ombudsman referral
escalation_paths.trigger: Internal escalation remains delayed or substantively incomplete
escalation_paths.likely_response: Request for complaint chronology, authority responses, and evidence of unresolved issue

linked_cases.note: Earlier case also involved staged institutional response and escalation readiness

structural_insight: The case shows institutional delay shifting into partial procedural response, with responsibility redirected rather than the substantive issue resolved.
personal_positioning: The complainant should keep the evidence record precise, distinguish partial acknowledgement from substantive remedy, and link each attachment to a specific unresolved target.
decision_note: Use the Admin Record Evidence view to verify evidence coverage for delay, partial response, responsibility redirection, repeated contact, and escalation readiness before external referral.
learning_capture: Partial institutional response can make a case appear active while leaving outcome, resolution, closure, and archive readiness incomplete unless the supporting evidence is linked target by target.
